---
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: ocs.scss
    transition: fade
    transition-speed: slow
    slide-number: true
    show-slide-number: print
    fig-cap-location: top
editor: source  
---



# Project context {data-background-color="#1b241f"}

## 

::::: columns
::: {.column width="35%"}

<br>

![](images/ocs-transparent2.png){width="100%" fig-align="right"}
:::

:::{.column width="10%"}

:::

::: {.column width="55%" style="font-size: 25px; text-align: center; margin: 0 auto;"}

<br>

-   <div style="margin-bottom: 1.5em;">[Social Cohesion Observatory (OCS)](https://ocs-coes.com/){target="_blank"} was a project funded under [Center for the Study of Conflict and Social Cohesion (COES 2013-2025)](https://coes.cl/){target="_blank"}</div>

-   <div style="margin-bottom: 1.5em;">Launched in 2020 with the aim of contributing to the analysis of social cohesion in Chile and Latin America</div>

-  <div style="margin-bottom: 1.5em;">It is based on experience gained from international projects focused on conceptualizing and measuring social cohesion</div>

- <div style="margin-bottom: 1.5em;">This study: based on data from VISLATAM (N = 179,377, nested within 174 country waves across 25 countries)</div>

:::
:::::



## {data-background-color="#1b241f"}

::: {.highlight-last style="font-size:45px; text-align:center; display:flex; flex-direction:column; justify-content:center; height:80vh; gap:2em;"}

*What have been the regional and national trends over the past two decades in the area of social cohesion in Latin America?*  

*What are the main factors associated with these changes?*

:::
